docs.title: mcpd
docs.text:
Run your agents, not your infrastructure.

mcpd is a toolchain and runtime developed by Mozilla AI that simplifies the configuration, execution and integration of Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers with your agentic application. It is intended to provide the same experience across local development and production environments.

It removes the friction of cross-language server orchestration, secrets management, version pinning, and lifecycle control.

docs.title: ✨ Why mcpd?
docs.text:
Traditional agent frameworks often embed complex subprocess logic, brittle startup scripts, and ad-hoc 'desktop style' config.

mcpd replaces this with:

🧪 Zero-Config Tool Setup No cloning repos or installing language-specific dependencies. mcpd add and mcpd daemon handle everything.

🌍 Language-Agnostic Tooling Use MCP servers written in Python (uvx), JavaScript/TypeScript (npx) in your code via a HTTP REST API that supports routing to MCP Servers.

🧭 Declarative Tool Management Define version-pinned MCP servers and tools in .mcpd.toml. Reproducible, consistent, and CI-friendly.

🔐 Project config separated from runtime variables Args and environment variables per server e.g. ~/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml. Never commit dev specific vars to Git again.

🛠️ Unified Dev Experience One command: mcpd daemon. Starts and manages all servers behind the scenes.

🧰 Intuitive SDK Integration The Python mcpd_sdk makes calling tools feel like native function calls; no HTTP, STDIO, or SSE boilerplate. Even easier for users of any-agent via .agent_tools().

🚀 Seamless Local-to-Prod Transition The same .mcpd.toml and agent code work in dev, CI, and cloud environments without modification.

docs.title: 🏗️ Built for Dev & Infra
docs.text: Feature Dev Workflow Production Benefit .mcpd.toml Version-controlled agent tool spec Declarative IaC for tools mcpd daemon Run everything locally Run in container alongside your agentic app mcpd config export Discover required vars Populate CI/CD pipelines Secure secrets store Local overrides per dev Injected via Control Plane Same binary Local builds Cloud deployment SDKs for Python Iterate locally Plug into prod orchestrators

docs.title: 🌐 Deploy Anywhere
docs.text:
mcpd is runtime-flexible and infrastructure-agnostic:

  • ⚙️ Works in any container or host with uv and npx (NodeJS)
  • ☁️ Multi-cloud ready (AWS, GCP, Azure, on-prem)
  • ♻️ Low resource overhead via in-process server management

docs.title: Homebrew
docs.text: brew install --cask mozilla-ai/tap/mcpd

docs.title: 📚 Explore the Docs
docs.text:
Use the sidebar to explore:

  • ✅ Requirements

docs.title: 🌐 About Mozilla AI
docs.text:
This project is built and maintained by Mozilla AI, a mission-driven organization reimagining AI for the public good.

Have ideas or feedback? Contributions welcome via GitHub.

docs.title: Global Configuration
docs.text:
Precedence

The order of precedence for these options is: CLI flag > environment variable > default value

docs.title: Config File Path
docs.text:
All commands support an optional parameter to specify the location of the mcpd config file.

You can provide this path in multiple ways:

  • CLI flag: --config-file <path>
  • Environment variable: MCPD_CONFIG_FILE=<path>
  • Default: .mcpd.toml in the current working directory

docs.title: Sample Configuration File
docs.text:
[[servers]]
  name = "fetch"
  package = "uvx::mcp-server-fetch@2025.4.7"

[[servers]]
  name = "time"
  package = "uvx::mcp-server-time@0.6.2"
  tools = ["get_current_time"]

docs.title: Log Level
docs.text:
Sets the logging level for mcpd.

You can configure it using:

  • CLI flag: --log-level=<level>
  • Environment variable: MCPD_LOG_LEVEL=<level>

Default:

INFO

docs.title: Log Path
docs.text:
Sets the log file path for mcpd.

Options:

  • CLI flag: --log-path=<path>
  • Environment variable: MCPD_LOG_PATH=<path>

Setting Log Path

Log entries will be discarded by default, unless a log path is configured. Output intended for the terminal is still emitted.

docs.title: Configuration Export
docs.text: The mcpd config export command generates portable configuration files for deployment across different environments. It creates template variables using the naming pattern MCPD__{SERVER_NAME}__{VARIABLE_NAME}.

docs.title: Template Variable Generation
docs.text:
Environment variables and command-line arguments are both converted to template variables using the same naming scheme:

  • Environment variableDATABASE_URL becomes MCPD__{SERVER_NAME}__DATABASE_URL
  • Command-line argument --database-url becomes MCPD__{SERVER_NAME}__DATABASE_URL

docs.title: Variable Name Collisions
docs.text:
Naming Collisions

If a server has both an environment variable and a command-line argument that normalize to the same name (e.g., DATABASE_URL and --database-url), they will generate the same template variable name.

In most cases, this is intentional, the same configuration value is being used in different ways. The collision results in a single template variable that can be used for both the environment variable and command-line argument.

docs.title: Example collision
docs.text:
[[servers]]
name = "example"
required_env = ["DATABASE_URL"]
required_args = ["--database-url"]

Both will use the template variable MCPD__EXAMPLE__DATABASE_URL in the generated files.

docs.title: Global Configuration
docs.text:
Precedence

The order of precedence for these options is: CLI flag > environment variable > default value

docs.title: Runtime File Path
docs.text:
All commands support an optional parameter to specify the location of the mcpd runtime file which provides the execution context.

You can provide this path in multiple ways:

  • CLI flag: --runtime-file <path>
  • Environment variable: MCPD_RUNTIME_FILE=<path>
  • Default: ~/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml

XDG_CONFIG_HOME environment variable

mcpd honors the XDG Base Directory Specification, respecting the XDG_CONFIG_HOME environment variable. This forms the base directory where mcpd will create an application folder.

The runtime file is modified using the following commands:

  • mcpd config args set
  • mcpd config env set

These values apply at runtime and are separate from your project-specific .mcpd.toml.

docs.title: Sample Configuration File
docs.text:
[servers]
  [servers.fetch]
    args = ["--ignore-robots-txt", "--user-agent=mcpd/1.0.0"]
    [servers.fetch.env]
      foo = "bar"
  [servers.time]
    args = ["--local-timezone=Europe/London"]
    [servers.time.env]
      baz = "123"
      qwerty = "xyz"

Manual Changes

The Execution Context Configuration file is automatically updated by mcpd config commands, you shouldn't edit it by hand unless absolutely necessary.

docs.title: Makefile Usage
docs.text:
The mcpd project includes a Makefile to streamline common developer tasks.

Running make

All commands should be run from the root of the repository.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
Environment

Most commands assume you have Go installed and available in your PATH.

docs.title: 🧱 Build
docs.text:
• Build the binary 

    make build

    Architectures and Operating Systems

    You can explicitly build the binary for a different architecture (amd64/arm64) or operating systems with:

    • make build-linux
    • make build-linux-arm64

  • Remove the compiled binary from the working directory 

    make clean

  • Install the binary to your system (typically /usr/local/bin) 

    sudo make install

    Dependency

    The install target relies on the standard build target.

  • Uninstall the binary 

    sudo make uninstall

docs.title: 🧪 Test
docs.text:
• Run all Go tests 
    make test

docs.title: 🐳 Run
docs.text:
• Start mcpd in a container 

    make local-up

    Default files

    By default the following files will be mounted to the container:

    • .mcpd.toml - the project configuration file in this repository
    • ~/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml - the default location for runtime configuration

  • Stop mcpd 

    make local-down

docs.title: 📝 Documentation
docs.text:
These commands manage the MkDocs developer documentation site for mcpd.

  • Generate CLI reference docs from the Cobra commands 

    make docs-cli

  • Update mkdocs.yaml navigation for the CLI commands 

    make docs-nav

  • Serve the docs locally using MkDocs + uv 

    make docs-local

  • Full pipeline: generate CLI docs, update nav, serve locally 

    make docs

    First time?

    The docs-local command will create a virtual environment using uv, install MkDocs + Material theme, and start the local server at http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: 🧭 Target Reference
docs.text:
Here’s a complete list of Makefile targets:

Target Description build Compile the Go binary build-linux Compile the Go binary for Linux on amd64 build-linux-arm64 Compile the Go binary for Linux on arm64 install Install binary to system path uninstall Remove installed binary clean Remove compiled binary from working directory test Run all Go tests local-up Start mcpd in a Docker container local-down Stop a running mcpd Docker container docs-cli Generate Markdown CLI reference docs docs-nav Update CLI doc nav in mkdocs.yaml docs-local Serve docs locally via mkdocs serve docs Alias for docs-local (runs everything)

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
To use mcpd, ensure the following tools are installed:

Tool Purpose Notes Docker Required if you want to run mcpd in a local container https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/ Go >= 1.24.4 Required for building mcpd and running tests https://go.dev/doc/install uv for running uvx Python packages in mcpd, and local docs https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/ npx for running JavaScript/TypeScript packages in mcpd https://docs.npmjs.com/downloading-and-installing-node-js-and-npm

Internet Connectivity

mcpd requires internet access to contact package registries and to allow MCP servers access to the internet if required when running.

docs.title: Basic Tutorial
docs.text: This tutorial walks you through using mcpd from setup to making requests to a running MCP server.

docs.title: 1. Build and Install
docs.text:
go version           # e.g. go version go1.24.4
make build           # Builds the binary
sudo make install    # Installs the binary to /usr/local/bin

docs.title: 2. Initialize the Project
docs.text:
mcpd init

Config File Location

This creates an .mcpd.toml file in your current directory.

docs.title: 3. Add an MCP Server
docs.text:
Add the latest version of the time server: 

mcpd add time

Or add a specific version and tool: 

mcpd add time --version 0.6.2 --tool get_current_time

docs.title: 4. Set Startup Arguments
docs.text:
Configure any startup flags required by the server: 

mcpd config args set time -- --local-timezone=Europe/London

docs.title: 5. Start the Daemon
docs.text:
Start mcpd, which launches MCP servers and exposes the HTTP API: 

mcpd daemon

API Endpoint

The API docs will be available at http://localhost:8090/docs

docs.title: 6. Query Running Servers
docs.text:
List all running servers: 

curl -s http://localhost:8090/api/v1/servers | jq

docs.title: 7. Call a Tool on a Server
docs.text:
Make a request to a tool on a specific MCP server: 

curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" \\
  -d '{"timezone": "America/New_York"}' \\
  http://localhost:8090/api/v1/servers/time/tools/get_current_time | jq

docs.title: Using mcpd in your Python application
docs.text: For tutorials on using mcpd with agents in Python, please refer to the Python mcpd SDK documentation.

docs.title: mcpd
docs.text: mcpd CLI is the primary interface for developers to interact with, and configure mcpd

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: The mcpd CLI is the primary interface for developers to interact with mcpd define their agent projects, and manage MCP server dependencies

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--config-file string    path to config file (default ".mcpd.toml")
      --log-level string      log level for mcpd logs (default "info")
      --log-path string       log file path to use for log output
      --runtime-file string   path to runtime (execution context) file that contains env vars, and arguments for your MCP servers (default "/home/runner/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml")

docs.title: SEE ALSO
docs.text:
• mcpd add - Adds an MCP server dependency to the project
  • mcpd config - Manages configuration for MCP servers
  • mcpd daemon - Launches an mcpd daemon instance
  • mcpd init - Initializes the current directory as an mcpd project
  • mcpd remove - Removes an MCP server dependency from the project
  • mcpd search - Searches all configured registries for matching MCP servers

docs.title: mcpd add
docs.text: Adds an MCP server dependency to the project

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Adds an MCP server dependency to the project. mcpd will search the registry for the named server and attempt to return information on the version specified, or 'latest' if no version specified

mcpd add <server-name> [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--format format      Specify the output format (one of: json, text, yaml) (default text)
      --runtime uvx        Optional, specify the runtime of the server package (e.g. uvx, `npx`)
      --source mcpm        Optional, specify the source registry of the server package (e.g. mcpm)
      --tool stringArray   Optional, when specified limits the available tools on the server (can be repeated)
      --version string     Specify the version of the server package (default "latest")

      --runtime-file string   path to runtime (execution context) file that contains env vars, and arguments for your MCP servers (default "/home/runner/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml")

docs.title: mcpd config export
docs.text: Exports current configuration, generating a pair of safe and portable configuration files

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Exports current configuration, generating a pair of safe and portable configuration files.

Using a project's required configuration (e.g. .mcpd.toml) and the locally configured runtime values from the execution context file (e.g. ~/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml), the export command outputs two files:

Environment Contract:

Lists all required and configured environment variables as secure, namespaced placeholders:

MCPD__{SERVER_NAME}__{VAR_NAME} - Creates placeholders for both environment variables and command line arguments to be populated with env vars

This file is intended for the platform operator or CI/CD system

Portable Execution Context:

  • A new secrets .toml file that defines sanitized runtime args and env sections for each server using the placeholders aligned with the environment contract
  • These files are safe to check into version control if required.

This allows running an mcpd project in any environment, cleanly separating the configuration structure from the secret values

mcpd config export [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--context-output string    Optional, specify the output path for the templated execution context config file (default "secrets.prod.toml")
      --contract-output string   Optional, specify the output path for the templated environment file (default ".env")
      --format format            Specify the format of the contract output file (one of: dotenv) (default dotenv)

docs.title: Options inherited from parent commands
docs.text:
--config-file string    path to config file (default ".mcpd.toml")
      --log-level string      log level for mcpd logs (default "info")
      --log-path string       log file path to use for log output
      --runtime-file string   path to runtime (execution context) file that contains env vars, and arguments for your MCP servers (default "/home/runner/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml")

docs.title: mcpd daemon
docs.text: Launches an mcpd daemon instance

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Launches an mcpd daemon instance, which starts MCP servers and provides routing via HTTP API

mcpd daemon [--dev] [--addr] [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--addr string   Address for the daemon to bind (not applicable in --dev mode) (default "0.0.0.0:8090")
      --dev           Run the daemon in development-focused mode

docs.title: Options inherited from parent commands
docs.text:
--config-file string    path to config file (default ".mcpd.toml")
      --log-level string      log level for mcpd logs (default "info")
      --log-path string       log file path to use for log output
      --runtime-file string   path to runtime (execution context) file that contains env vars, and arguments for your MCP servers (default "/home/runner/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml")

docs.title: SEE ALSO
docs.text: • mcpd - mcpd CLI is the primary interface for developers to interact with, and configure mcpd

docs.title: mcpd init
docs.text: Initializes the current directory as an mcpd project

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Initializes the current directory as an mcpd project, creating a .mcpd.toml configuration file.

This command sets up the basic structure required for an mcpd project.

The configuration file path can be overridden using the --config-file flag or the MCPD_CONFIG_FILE environment variable

mcpd init [flags]

docs.title: Options inherited from parent commands
docs.text:
--config-file string    path to config file (default ".mcpd.toml")
      --log-level string      log level for mcpd logs (default "info")
      --log-path string       log file path to use for log output
      --runtime-file string   path to runtime (execution context) file that contains env vars, and arguments for your MCP servers (default "/home/runner/.config/mcpd/secrets.dev.toml")

docs.title: SEE ALSO
docs.text: • mcpd - mcpd CLI is the primary interface for developers to interact with, and configure mcpd

docs.title: mcpd remove
docs.text: Removes an MCP server dependency from the project

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Removes an MCP server dependency from the project

mcpd remove <server-name> [flags]

docs.title: mcpd search
docs.text: Searches all configured registries for matching MCP servers

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Searches all configured registries for matching MCP servers, when name is not specified, the wildcard character (*) is used. Returns aggregated results from all configured registries

mcpd search [server-name] [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--category stringArray   Optional, specify a partial match for required categories (can be repeated)
      --format format          Specify the output format (one of: json, text, yaml) (default text)
      --license string         Optional, specify a partial match for the license of the server package (e.g. MIT, Apache)
      --official               Optional, only official server packages are included in results
      --runtime string         Optional, specify the runtime of the server package (e.g. uvx, npx)
      --source string          Optional, specify the source registry of the server package (e.g. mcpm)
      --tag stringArray        Optional, specify a partial match for required tags (can be repeated)
      --tool stringArray       Optional, specifies the tools the server must expose (can be repeated)
      --version string         Optional, specify the version of the server package

docs.title: Options inherited from parent commands
docs.text:
--config-file string    path to config file (default ".mcpd.toml")
      --log-level string      log level for mcpd logs (default "info")
